messages.author: JDat
messages.text: I am doing some experiments on RP2040 (without WiFi) to remap LoRA pins and change from SPI1 to SPI (SPI0) in "rpi_picow" variant. So far everything works great using USB serial and Chrome web app. Maybe in future I'll switch to RP2350 and do some experiments with it.
messages.content_html: I am doing some experiments on RP2040 (without WiFi) to remap LoRA pins and change from SPI1 to SPI (SPI0) in "rpi_picow" variant. So far everything works great using USB serial and Chrome web app. Maybe in future I'll switch to RP2350 and do some experiments with it.
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: JDat
messages.text: OK. I got all four variants from UK (2040/2350/noWiFI/withWifi). After some time I'll start experiments with 2350, then with WiFi/BT. So, patience, it RP2350 will work with Meshcore. (edited)
messages.content_html: OK. I got all four variants from UK (2040/2350/noWiFI/withWifi). After some time I'll start experiments with 2350, then with WiFi/BT. So, patience, it RP2350 will work with Meshcore. (edited)

messages.author: JDat
messages.text: For me there is no such thing as RP2040 version. So far I played with RP2040 without WiFi, but... Boards? Original from Farnell. And Waveshare clone from Aliexpress. Difference? Board size and power chain. Anyway I am soldering SX1262 radio modules on Pico boards myself. Three different variants each have own pinout. One RPi-picow right from github. Then variants where I am using SPI0 instead of SPI1(default).
messages.content_html: For me there is no such thing as RP2040 version. So far I played with RP2040 without WiFi, but... Boards? Original from Farnell. And Waveshare clone from Aliexpress. Difference? Board size and power chain. Anyway I am soldering SX1262 radio modules on Pico boards myself. Three different variants each have own pinout. One RPi-picow right from github. Then variants where I am using SPI0 instead of SPI1(default).

messages.author: JDat
messages.text: V1.11 repeater works for ~12 days... Until battery drain out... I has patched source code in variant to get working with SPI0 and custom pinout. Right now I have problems with V1.12 repeater firmware. Getting radio init error -2 RADIOLIB_ERR_CHIP_NOT_FOUND Radio chip was not found during initialization. This can be caused by specifying wrong chip type in the constructor (i.e. calling SX1272 constructor for SX1278 chip) or by a fault in your wiring (incorrect slave select pin). Preparing V1.13 for experiments and tests for tomorrow. Maybe... If necessary, I'll brutally remove in V1.13 repeater source code following parts: UI (display), EnvironmentSensors, RTC chip support, I2C (Wire), any strange digitalWrite(), sleeping etc code from source. Sometimes I am getting feeling that script kiddies mess with generic platform independent code too much and breaking something with their zillion of pull requests just to reduce power consumption by "toggling GPIO pin for display". (edited)
messages.content_html: V1.11 repeater works for ~12 days... Until battery drain out... I has patched source code in variant to get working with SPI0 and custom pinout. Right now I have problems with V1.12 repeater firmware. Getting radio init error -2 RADIOLIB_ERR_CHIP_NOT_FOUND Radio chip was not found during initialization. This can be caused by specifying wrong chip type in the constructor (i.e. calling SX1272 constructor for SX1278 chip) or by a fault in your wiring (incorrect slave select pin). Preparing V1.13 for experiments and tests for tomorrow. Maybe... If necessary, I'll brutally remove in V1.13 repeater source code following parts: UI (display), EnvironmentSensors, RTC chip support, I2C (Wire), any strange digitalWrite(), sleeping etc code from source. Sometimes I am getting feeling that script kiddies mess with generic platform independent code too much and breaking something with their zillion of pull requests just to reduce power consumption by "toggling GPIO pin for display". (edited)
messages.reactions_html: 1
messages.reply_html:
Mike's Allotment
Thanks for the clarification - in any of your experiments have to ever seen the RP2040 +sx1262 as a Repeater work absolutely perfectly but then just completely hang after about 10 seconds?

messages.author: JDat
messages.text: Possible reasons why not working: some kind of problematic digitalWrite() Serial.printf() and firends or I2S into my SPI pins. Maybe because something missing in cofig and default pins invoked or intentional hardware dependent code injection into platform independent code. (edited)
messages.content_html: Possible reasons why not working: some kind of problematic digitalWrite() Serial.printf() and firends or I2S into my SPI pins. Maybe because something missing in cofig and default pins invoked or intentional hardware dependent code injection into platform independent code. (edited)

messages.author: JDat
messages.text: Yes and no, in superposition! I am speaking too much about RP2040, but at the same time there are problems when using RP platform in general. Lot of Rp2040 problems apply to RP2350... In theory... But, yes, if somebody can make new thread... It would be great!
